title: Worktree state.json mutations must reach the canonical file: apply-decisions severs the symlink (worktree.go:137) and cycle_finalize has no copy-back — plus the ACS Goodhart gap that verified the dead copy
why: LIVE INCIDENT cycle-999 (audit-report defects): the carryover-convergence task correctly mutated 135->14 in its WORKTREE state.json, but apply-decisions --apply severed the worktree symlink to the host-canonical file (worktree.go:137), stranding the mutation in a detached gitignored copy (ledger.jsonl symlink stayed intact — proven asymmetry); cycle_finalize.go:37 never copies state.json back, so convergence never reached the live system. Compounding: the cycle's ACS suite asserted against the WORKTREE copy and passed green while the live target was unmet — evaluation must always target the canonical/live surface the goal names.
how_to_apply: Root-cause both halves: (1) PROPAGATION — either apply-decisions must mutate THROUGH the symlink (never replace-the-link; write via the statemap RMW on the resolved canonical path, composing with statejson-stalerevision-cas-lost-write), or cycle finalize gains an explicit reconcile step for state.json worktree deltas; pick symlink-preserving write as primary (single canonical writer path, no divergent copies). (2) EVAL TARGET — ACS predicates for live-state goals must resolve the CANONICAL path (git-common-dir anchored, like cycle-984's predicates) not the worktree copy; add a predicate-authoring rule + a lint that flags state.json assertions on worktree-relative paths. Wiring proof: composed test — worktree apply mutates canonical, symlink intact afterward, ACS predicate reads canonical.
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-999/audit-report.md defects[]; worktree.go:137; cycle_finalize.go:37; contrast cycle-984 predicates (git-common-dir, correct target).
